qa_pairs.question: What are modules on our servers, and what is the main benefit of this modular architecture?
qa_pairs.answer: On our servers, modules are configuration files that contain instructions for modifying your software environment. The main benefit of this modular architecture is that it allows multiple versions of the same application or library to be installed and used without conflict.

qa_pairs.question: How do users find available software and load a specific module version on our servers?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find available modules by using the `module spider` command to search the complete tree of all modules, or `module avail` to list modules they can load. To load a specific module, such as a compiler suite, users execute the `module load <module-name>` command; for instance, `module load gcc/9.3`.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user check which modules are currently active in their environment, and what command removes all active modules at once?
qa_pairs.answer: To check which modules are currently active in your environment, you would use the `module list` command. To remove all currently loaded modules with a single command, you can use `module purge`.
